question: Suppose you have the following series of queue operations. What items are left on the queue?
code:
q = Queue()
q.enqueue("hello")
q.enqueue("dog")
q.enqueue(3)
q.dequeue()

answers.feedback: Correct! A queue follows the First-In, First-Out (FIFO) principle. The sequence of operations works as follows: 1. enqueue("hello") adds "hello" to the front. 2. enqueue("dog") adds "dog" behind it. 3. enqueue(3) adds 3 to the rear. 4. dequeue() removes the item at the front, which was "hello". This leaves "dog" and 3 in the queue.
Incorrect. This would happen if the dequeue operation removed the last item added (LIFO behavior), which is characteristic of a stack, not a queue.
Incorrect. This is the state of the queue before the dequeue operation was performed. The dequeue operation must remove one element from the front.
Incorrect. This would imply that two dequeue operations were performed, or that the queue only kept the most recent entry.
